tools.name: alert
tools.description: CRUD API for IP Defender alerts (trademark watch alerts). Create, read, update, delete, or list alerts. Each alert monitors a brand name across selected countries and reports conflicting trademark applications.

tools.name: billing
tools.description: Retrieve billing information for the authenticated user: current daily service rate and account status (op='rate'), the list of invoices (op='invoices'), or a single invoice with line items and buyer details (op='invoiceDetail').

tools.name: conflict
tools.description: List, read, or update IP Defender conflict records (conflicting trademark applications detected by an alert). Returns per-state counts and full conflict details including the conflicting mark's source, image, and match pairs.

tools.name: echo
tools.description: MCP debug tool: echoes the `message` argument back to the client. Useful as a smoke test for the MCP gateway. Use only for MCP debugging. Do not use under normal circumstances.

tools.name: getPricingList
tools.description: List all territories with pricing info for monitoring trademark in those territories. Billed daily, You only pay for active monitoring in your selected markets, with usage tracked on a daily basis for maximum flexibility. Your first statement arrives 30 days after activation, followed by quarterly b

tools.name: settings
tools.description: Get or set the authenticated user's account settings (notification emails, billing emails, invoice address, given/family name, company details, etc.). op='get' reads requested properties; op='set' writes provided property values.

title: Tool description changed since last scan
detail: Tool "echo" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "(hash 66abbb14)" → after: "MCP debug tool: echoes the `message` argument back to the client. Useful as a smoke test for the MCP gateway. Use only for MCP debugging. Do not use under normal circumstances."

remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.
